_meta.purpose: Wild-encounter LEVEL bands per grind-relevant area in FireRed — the game-knowledge layer (rule 14) for a LEVEL-AWARE grind-spot picker. The problem it serves (documented NS#1/#14 wall): a grind() on grass whose wild_max is FAR BELOW the team's grind target gives near-zero XP, so the grind spins its whole budget for ~0 levels (the E4-prep L45->55 stall on Route-18/low grass). A picker reads this to (a) recognise a map as GRIND-INADEQUATE for the current target and (b) prefer a reachable area whose band actually levels the team.
_meta.source: Bulbapedia FRLG route/cave encounter tables + long-standing FRLG knowledge, distilled offline (rule 15). Bands are the APPROXIMATE min..max of the common land-grass (or cave step) encounters used for grinding — not exact per-slot tables; the picker only needs 'is the XP here worth it for a mon at level L'. Water/surf-only and fishing bands are noted separately where they dominate.
_meta.schema: spots: { "<group>,<num>": {"name": str, "wild_min": int, "wild_max": int, "terrain": "grass"|"cave"|"water", "gate": null|str} }. gate = a human note on why the spot may be unreachable early (badge/HM/quest); the picker still gates reachability via the world graph — this is only a hint. Map-ids follow campaign._PLACE_NAMES (Route N = (3,18+N); Route 21=(3,39/40); 22=(3,41); 23=(3,42); 24=(3,43); 25=(3,44)).
_meta.updated: 2026-07-11 (night-shift #5, PASS-3 grind-efficiency lever a — KB + reader landed; picker wiring is the verify-gated next step)
